games.name: Counter-Strike 2
games.note: Native Linux client; full competitive matchmaking.

games.name: Dota 2
games.note: Native Linux client.

games.name: Team Fortress 2
games.note: Native Linux client.

games.name: Baldur's Gate 3
games.note: Native Linux client. Proton is also worth testing for regressions.

games.name: The Witcher 3
games.note: Native Linux client included.

games.name: Hollow Knight
games.note: Native Linux client.

games.name: Stardew Valley
games.note: Native Linux client.

games.name: Terraria
games.note: Native Linux client.

games.name: Elden Ring
games.note: Platinum-rated community reports; online path is part of KythOS validation.

games.name: Cyberpunk 2077
games.note: Excellent Proton support. Test HDR, FSR, and frame pacing on each release.

games.name: Red Dead Redemption 2
games.note: Gold community reports; Rockstar Launcher remains the risk surface.

games.name: GTA V
games.note: Story mode and launcher path need periodic revalidation.

games.name: God of War
games.note: Platinum-rated community reports.

games.name: Hogwarts Legacy
games.note: Gold-rated community reports.

games.name: Spider-Man Remastered
games.note: Gold/Platinum community reports.

games.name: Resident Evil 4 (2023)
games.note: Works via Proton.

games.name: Sekiro
games.note: Platinum community reports.

games.name: Dark Souls III
games.note: Works via Proton; VAC does not restrict Linux for this title.

games.name: Monster Hunter: World
games.note: Gold community reports.

games.name: Hades
games.note: Platinum community reports.

games.name: Deep Rock Galactic
games.note: EAC Linux support is enabled; retest after major anti-cheat updates.

games.name: Dead by Daylight
games.note: EAC Linux support is enabled; retest after major game updates.

games.name: The Finals
games.note: EAC Linux path has community support; validate matchmaking on release images.

games.name: Halo Infinite
games.note: EAC Linux support is enabled.

games.name: Overwatch 2
games.note: Runs via Proton + Battle.net through Lutris/umu. Launcher flow is the main risk.

games.name: Paladins
games.note: EAC Linux support is enabled by the publisher.

games.name: Rainbow Six Siege
games.note: Mostly works; occasional BattlEye launch failures. Try PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 if it fails to start.

games.name: Hunt: Showdown
games.note: BattlEye enabled but reports are mixed. Check ProtonDB before promising support.

games.name: Warframe
games.note: EAC Linux support enabled; occasionally needs a specific Proton version.

games.name: Apex Legends
games.note: EA removed Steam Deck/Linux support in 2024. Retest only if EA announces a policy reversal.

games.name: Destiny 2
games.note: Bungie states SteamOS/Proton is unsupported and warns against bypass attempts.

games.name: Rust
games.note: Facepunch has publicly said there are no current Proton/Linux support plans.

games.name: PUBG
games.note: Treat as blocked until KythOS validation confirms current official Proton support.

games.name: Valorant
games.note: Riot Vanguard requires ring-0 kernel access: Windows-only driver. No Linux support from Riot.

games.name: Fortnite
games.note: Epic has not enabled Linux support for Fortnite, despite EAC supporting Linux for other games.

games.name: Call of Duty (MP/WZ)
games.note: RICOCHET anti-cheat runs at kernel level on Windows and has no Linux support.

games.name: Roblox
games.note: Roblox's Byfron/Hyperion anti-cheat blocks Wine/Proton paths.

games.name: League of Legends
games.note: Riot migrated LoL to Vanguard in 2024. Same Linux blocker as Valorant.

games.name: Escape from Tarkov
games.note: BattlEye can support Linux, but this title has not enabled a supported Proton path.

games.name: Lost Ark
games.note: nProtect GameGuard is kernel-level and blocks Linux.
